page_content:
Formerly: SOCY498C.

SOCY452 Sociology of Mental Health (3 Credits)

The class focuses upon the larger question: "What is the balance between people being mentally ‘ill’ and us having a'sick' society?” To explore this question, students will utilize sociological approaches toward mental illness and health. This will manifest as a focus on how social organization is related to mental health and illness. Students will consider how sociologists understand the nature, distribution, and treatment of mental illness and health in society, and will develop a personal understanding of what it means to be mentally healthy. Prerequisite: 6 credits in SOCY courses; or permission of the department. Credit Only Granted for. SOCY4980 or SOCY 452.
